about:
Chuck Eesley ("ease-lee") is an Associate Professor and W.M. Keck Foundation Faculty Scholar in the Department of Management Science and Engineering at Stanford University. He is also a Faculty Director of the Stanford Technology Ventures Program and a faculty affiliate at the Stanford Center for AI Safety (SAFE) where his work explores the intersection of artificial intelligence entrepreneurship and platform governance. His recent research focuses on how AI/ML algorithms shape opportunity access and performance in platform-mediated entrepreneurship as well as the role of digital platforms in financing and spreading misinformation.

Prof. Eesley’s broader research examines how institutional and university environments influence high-growth engineering-driven entrepreneurship. He studies how education and policy can better support the economic and entrepreneurial outcomes of engineering students and alumni. His field research spans China Japan South Korea Chile Bangladesh Uganda Ethiopia Thailand and Silicon Valley and has received awards from the Schulze Foundation the Technical University of Munich and the Kauffman Foundation.

He serves on the Steering Committee for the Stanford King Center on Global Development and the Advisory Committee for the Stanford Center at Peking University. He is also a faculty affiliate at the Stanford Center on China’s Economy and Institutions and the Woods Institute for the Environment and he serves on the Editorial Board of the Strategic Management Journal. His work has been published in Nature Organization Science Strategic Management Journal and Biological Psychiatry among others.

A former entrepreneur and investor Prof. Eesley has been a mentor and advocate for immigrants and first-generation low-income students in STEM and entrepreneurship through initiatives such as the AAAS Global Innovation through Science and Technology (GIST) program. He earned his Ph.D. at the M.I.T. Sloan School of Management and a B.S. in neuroscience from Duke University.

NovoEd.com launched around his online course which was the first entrepreneurship MOOC and has taught over 200 000 students in over 100 countries.
company_description: Stanford Engineering has been at the forefront of innovation for a century creating pivotal technologies that have transformed the world. The school conducts research that continues to have a significant impact on society and trains the engineers of the future to meet the challenges of the 21st century. With nine departments several institutes and many labs and centers the school focuses on a multidisciplinary approach to research teaching and problem-solving.

about:
Midlife is Not a Crisis—It’s Your Greatest Opportunity. 
For too long women over 40 have been told that midlife is the beginning of the end...of careers opportunities and relevance. I call BS on that. Midlife is actually the most powerful time in a woman’s life. With experience wisdom and ambition at its peak this is the moment to reinvent rediscover and reimagine what’s possible. 

As the author of Piloting Your Life a speaker coach and entrepreneur I encourage women to take control of their next chapter on their terms. I help ambitious accomplished women redefine success embrace reinvention and step into leadership with confidence.

 I believe in action not just inspiration.
I don’t just talk about midlife reinvention—I live it. My work is about equipping women with the roadmap strategies and mindset shifts needed to reclaim their power in career finances and personal growth. Whether it’s pivoting careers launching a business stepping into thought leadership or breaking barriers in male-dominated industries I help women break free from outdated narratives and build a future that excites them.

Who I Help:
 Women 40+ navigating career transitions reinvention or entrepreneurship 
 Leaders and executives who are done playing small and are ready to own their power
 Organizations and event planners looking for high-impact transformative speakers
 Podcasts and media outlets seeking an engaging guest to discuss midlife reinvention

How I Help:
 Author & Thought Leader – My book Piloting Your Life is a manifesto for midlife reinvention
 Speaking & Workshops – Empowering women at conferences leadership events and retreats
 Coaching & Consulting – Helping women navigate midlife transitions with confidence and clarity
 Podcast Guest – Available for interviews on topics related to reinvention midlife transitions and women's leadership

Midlife is the Launchpad for Something Bigger.
This is not the time to shrink. It’s time to amplify your voice step into leadership and rewrite your story. The world needs more powerful confident financially independent women who refuse to settle. If you’re done waiting for permission let’s make midlife your most exciting chapter yet.

 Pilot your own life...it’s time to take the controls and take control.
 Put yourself first...you’re not at the back of the line anymore.

 Ready to take off? Let’s connect.

about:
I’m a Harvard/UC Berkeley-trained scientist and translational physician who abandoned a surgical career to become a CEO. I did it because of my abuela Sylvia. Growing up I was close to my grandmother and witnessed her struggle to understand when her COPD was exacerbating. Home lung monitoring technologies are inaccurate or too challenging to use leaving patients and doctors to rely only on symptoms. As a result my grandmother died. Her experience with COPD inspired me to found Samay (“to breathe deeply” in Quechua) so that no one else should die because of misdiagnosis.

I love solving complex problems. I excel at applying my analytical skills to find unexpected connections among diverse disciplines. My life exemplifies this: I’m a Colombia Presidential* and National Merit Scholar** (top 0.1% Valedictorian) started college at 16 and graduated at the top of my class at Pontificia Universidad Javeriana my country’s #1 medical school. After a 4-year Harvard postdoc and a brief stint in plastic surgery at UChicago I re-trained in global health and translational medicine at the top three U.S. public schools: U Washington UC Berkeley and UCSF. I’ve co-led NIH-Gates-funded projects published in NEJM Nature PNAS and Genetics in Medicine received $3 million in awards raised $2.3M from investors and was granted an “Extraordinary Abilities” green card just four years after relocating to the US (average is 8+).

Samay combines lung physiology IoT sensors signal processing and AI/ML. My enduring passion for helping others motivates me to embrace any level of complexity. I enjoy questioning in service of my mission something that practicing clinical medicine didn't encourage. So I envision Samay as having a culture where people relish problems as gifts of discovery where fearless nonconformists are driven by social purpose. Someone with my energy level is equipped to market our invention.

I advocate for social justice as I've experienced discrimination in the past. I'm involved in initiatives that support underrepresented minorities in STEM and entrepreneurship. I come from a family of physicians but I’m married to a Googler engineer and have four balls of joy as pets. At 42 I had my first child after a 4-year-long IVF journey (six retrievals + five transfers). As an immigrant I know courage and persistence lead to positive outcomes. That’s the core of everything I do.

If you want to become part of our team partner or invest e-mail me at maria@samayhealth.com: happy to schedule an intro call.

*Bachiller por Colombia
**Andrés Bello Scholar
company_description:
Samay has cracked the code on respiratory diagnostics with Sylvee—our AI-enabled chest sensor that outperforms existing tools by 2x. Protected by 12 patents and backed by $5M+ from NSF NIH VCs and pharma partners Sylvee achieves breakthrough accuracy: 90%+ precision in COPD detection and 80% accuracy in air trapping identification.

Our integrated platform delivers what the market desperately needs: early COPD diagnosis continuous remote monitoring and biomarker discovery for 550+ million patients worldwide—80% of whom remain undiagnosed.

The impact? 

Preventing millions of premature deaths while reducing the $143B annual COPD expenses through partnerships with healthcare systems and pharmaceutical companies.
headline: CEO Samay NSF & NIH PI Global Winner MedTech Innovator ‘24 Bloomberg’s 100 Innovators in Latin America ’24 Inc. Magazin

about:
Like other angel investor organizations Bay Angels is comprised primarily of former entrepreneurs X-CxO and eco-system active members. Our members understand the challenges and complexities of launching a new business thru own experiences in startups SMB and Large Ent. Our style of mentoring involvement begins with a thorough evaluation and due diligence process. Then we provide needed counseling during the critical launch phase. Many of our members continue providing consultation throughout the early years of a new businesses' growth to ensure its success. Our in-depth investing process increases the likelihood of success—for the company and our investors. Bay Angels is managed by Jordan Wahbeh and Founded by Roger King
For newly established companies already showing significant progress Bay Angels provides the necessary funding to help them reach the next stage of their growth. Our investors have helped infuse dozens of early stage companies with the capital necessary to accelerate their success.
The opportunities for investment in the coming years will be exciting as entirely new technologies and industries emerge. Be part of helping shape the 21st Century. The best is yet to come.
The Bay Angels. Creating the future now.


To submit your company for funding - use this link

http://bit.ly/338g6fC
current_position_description:
Early stage startups—and established companies seeking expansion funding—that meet certain criteria are eligible for consideration by Bay Angels.
Investment size
Typically our members invest between $25k and $200k individually for round sizes of $300k-3M. For companies with especially attractive prospects several members may invest to enable the desired funding to be completed.
Stage
Unlike many angel organizations Bay Angels will consider early stage startups. We often provide the first outside investor money that a company raises. We also fund expansion rounds for companies with demonstrated market traction.
Capital efficiency
We look for companies with high potential to generate or expand sales revenue with a relatively modest amount of capital. Prime BayAngel investor targets are efficiently run new companies that are able to use the funding to increase revenue and operational functionality.
Target Industries
While most Bay Area angel and VC funding organizations focus primarily on technology BayAngels will consider a broad range of industries. We like companies in major industries in large or expanding target markets—and possess highly scalable business models. Companies that address major problems for large (or potentially large) target markets that can generate significant demand are highly desirable. However we generally avoid participating with companies that have long investment and exit cycles such as biotech and hardware.

The team: Roger King Jordan Wahbeh and Karim Nurani
company_description:
We invest in technology companies in seed angels pre-A and A stages.

Bay Angels was founded in 1998 by Roger King and managed by Philip King. We are Bay Area angel organization whose​ members include both accredited investors and members of the venture capital community. Over the years we've helped a number of early stage companies acquire funding angel funding and launch their offerings. One of our earlier success stories was OpenTable.

company_description:
Evvy is unlocking precision women's healthcare through novel overlooked female biomarkers — starting with the vaginal microbiome. 

Backed by leading healthcare investors such as General Catalyst Left Lane Capital and LabCorp the Evvy team includes leading OB/GYNs and vaginal microbiome researchers with decades of experience at organizations like UCSF Stanford Harvard Cleveland Clinic and more. Evvy is one of the fastest growing women's health companies in the US — enabling us to deliver innovative high quality care while scaling novel R&D to unlock a new data-driven future in women's health.

Evvy's Vaginal Health Test is the first at-home vaginal microbiome test to use metagenomic sequencing to tell you what's up down there why it matters and what you can do about it. Evvy's larger platform is the first vaginal healthcare service to bring together state-of-the-art testing precision clinical care and 1:1 coaching to give women and people with vaginas the care they deserve. Through this platform Evvy is building real-world datasets that can transform our understanding of complex female health conditions. 

Imagine a world where female health is finally understood: where risks are detected early and precisely conditions are defined with specificity and nuance and diseases are treated with personalization and care. That's the world that Evvy is pioneering.

about:
Is there a better alternative to AgroChemicals that doesn't damage your soil? 

For the past 75 years most growers have been using expensive chemical fertilizers and pesticides. Unfortunately this has destroyed soil fertility and reduced the flavor and nutrient density of crops. 

In fact most farmers find they need more chemicals each year in order to maintain the levels of output. For cannabis growers this also means a drop in THC and terpene levels.

Fortunately for forward-thinking growers a better understanding of soil biology has created an opportunity to restore soil health. SymSoil is at the forefront of creating products that feed plants the way nature intended and as a result will:

 Increase Yield

 Reduce Plant Pathogens & Pests

 Boost THC & Terpene Levels

 Reduce Operating Costs

 Improve Plant Healthy & Vitality

 Overall Increase Crop Quality

Complex or full-spectrum soil biology creates complex chemistry in the plant. SymSoil's Grow Cubes have the same biology as our Robust Compost with over 2 000 species including 184 families of fungi protozoa and beneficial nematodes. Each cube has approximately 5 000 beneficial fungi spores that enhance nutrient cycling.

Sym Soil is the first company to patent a process for large-scale production of biologically active compost that meets Soil Food Web specifications. 

The benefits for farmers are major: 

 Substantial increase in yield and quality of crops and 

 Creating a healthier more sustainable environment. 

Sym Soil believes there is an opportunity to create value for growers using Dr. Elaine Ingham's Soil Food Web concepts. Our total products and services include: 

 Grow Cubes (Ideal Cloning Medium)

 On-Farm Composting Service

 Robust Compost Product

 Soil Testing Lab

Feel free to learn more about what makes our approach unique at www.SymSoil.com

Connect with me here on LinkedIn via direct message email me at elizabethp@SymSoil.com or call 415-595-4784.
current_position_description: Elizabeth leads SymSoil through strategic and functional areas including business development. SymSoil offers premium quality living soil for farmers arborists and master gardeners at all size operations across the country.
company_description: The company Sym Soil Inc. is committed to soil health. We focus on microbial diversity by recolonizing native soil biomes. Our biologically enhanced living soil amendment like humus dramatically improves nutrient availability soil porosity and water retention.
headline: Soil & Compost Maven Increase Yield & Quality With Our Biologically Enriched Grow Cubes On-Farm Composting

Because competitive advantage is a who not a what. Great talent isn’t just an asset — it’s THE moat. 

While our specialty is recruiting startup-capable GTM Finance and Cross-Functional Product Leaders return clients frequently ask us to help fill other business-critical VP and C-level roles across Operations and HR. We’ve also taken on some of the toughest searches in the market—projects where other recruiters have tried and failed.

We’ve built our reputation on being:

• Extremely thorough
• Outcome-oriented within compressed timelines
• Unorthodox in how we price source pitch and recruit
• Uniquely effective at pre-onboarding new hires during the recruiting process

Our battle-tested workflow mitigates hiring risk secures the top 10% of talent and cuts both new client onboarding and new hire onboarding time in half.

Over the past 21 years—as a 5x startup founder angel investor GTM advisor direct response marketer sales leader husband and father—I’ve discovered my superpower is being an activator and connector of people. That’s what I spend my days doing and I love it.

The environment where I thrive is one that values:

• Unconventional thinking
• Direct communication
• Speed collaboration and transparency
• Empathy and loyalty
• Quality over quantity
• Data-driven iteration
• Learning humility laughter and mutual respect

Outside of work you’ll usually find me:

• Exploring nature with my wife our two kids and our two dogs
• Pushing limits at CrossFit getting bendy on my yoga mat or carving on my electric skateboard
• Kiteboarding climbing ocean cliffs (no ropes needed!) jumping out of a perfectly good airplane
• Or diving into a great book or podcast (shoutout to the legends of the All-In and Moonshots Pod!) 

Currently reading: "21 Lessons for the 21st Century” by Yuval Noah Harari “Principles" by Ray Dalio and "The Future Is Faster Than You Think” by Peter Diamandis

Mobile: (858) 779-4769
Email: kraig@bearhugrecruiting.com

company_description:
Bearhug Recruiting is a boutique executive search firm focused on inflection-point human architecture.

We partner with two distinct ecosystems:

• Founder-led Human Performance Brands scaling from ~$30M to $300M+
• Environmental Tech startups with PMF scaling up from $3M to $30M+

In both environments the pattern is familiar.

The business has momentum. The stakes rise. The margin for error shrinks.

The next phase will not be unlocked by effort alone. It will be unlocked by the right leadership.

Our work spans Digital Ecommerce Growth Commercial Product Finance and C-suite roles. From building structured product development organizations at $80M outdoor brands to installing first-time GTM leaders in Seed-stage AI companies preparing to commercialize we help founders strengthen the leadership architecture before the next growth phase or capital event.

Every search begins from zero. Full market mapping. Structured intake and calibration. Disciplined evaluation. High-touch engagement with leaders who are not actively looking and who ignore generic outreach.

Our Performance Partnership model ties incentives to outcomes. Milestone-based billing. Limited capacity. No more than three active searches per partner.

We do not recycle networks. We build the team required for what comes next.

Competitive advantage is still a who not a what.

Explore recent placements and learn more about our Easy Button MSA at:

www.BearhugRecruiting.com/services

LaunchPad Central:
LaunchPad Central integrates generative AI into its renowned Innovation Operating System GLIDR to accelerate global innovation for academic government and commercial teams. My name is Clare Matschullat and I am the founder and CEO of Cancer Sherpa. I know the pain fear and fury that brought you here as I am also a cancer survivor. I entered college and adulthood as a cancer survivor and have spent the past twenty-five years building the network and skills to empower cancer patients caretakers and survivors to seek the best cancer and follow-up care available to them. 

Whether you just received a preliminary diagnosis need assistance caring for a loved one with cancer or want to join a survivor support group I will help you thrive not merely survive. I wrote and live by my company’s mission: “(I) will advocate for you until you are able to advocate for yourself.”

At Cancer Sherpa we believe that every patient should benefit from our collective knowledge and resources. about:
Anne DeGheest pioneered the HealthTech or Digital Health space as a healthcare executive an entrepreneur angel investor corporate advisor and mentor capitalist. She is the founder of HealthTech Capital and MedStars. She is a recognized leader on how to successfully build innovative HealthTech businesses and change healthcare delivery. 

Since she founded MedStars in 1986 she has been a corporate development advisor to large healthcare companies and a mentor capitalist specializing in investing and mentoring early-stage companies in healthcare. Her life science portfolio companies have generated over $20 billion in market capitalization including 9 IPOs. She provided angel capital hands-on operating expertise and/or business strategy in the founding of major venture-backed companies like Pyxis (CareFusion) Aspect Medical (acquired by Covidien) VISX (acquired by AMO) Thermage (merged into Solta Medical) VISICU(acquired by Philips) Masimo (Nasdaq: MASI) Omada health (OMDA). She also helped grow new entrepreneurial divisions of large corporations like ASP/Johnson & Johnson Baxter Medtronic and McKesson. She was also part of the founding executive team of Nellcor (a division of Covidien) MedPool and OmniCell (Nasdaq: OMCL). She was an Entrepreneur-In-Residence with Canaan Partners and IVP.

Anne founded in 2010 HealthTech Capital a group of private investors dedicated to funding and mentoring new "HealthTech" startups to decrease healthcare cost and empower patients. HealthTech capital is an ecosystem of angels venture capitalists providers payors and healthcare industry key players with the three sets of expertise needed to succeed in this new HealthTech space: healthcare technology and user engagement.

Anne is also a recognized artist (www.adegheest.com). She is a frequent speaker at industry conferences and a guest-lecturer at Stanford Business School. She has an MBA from Harvard and a master's in business engineering from University of Brussels.
current_position_description: HealthTech Capital is the established leader in mentoring and investing in early stage HealthTech companies. Our members include private investors venture capitalists and industry players.
current_position_2_description:
MedStars specializes in working with large corporations and young entrepreneurs in starting and developing disruptive life sciences companies with new innovative and revolutionary products. Our markets include medical devices healthcare information systems health related consumer products and biotechnology. 

We have worked with some of the leading medical and biotech companies to help them develop their Healthtech strategy and launch their own breakthrough products. 

 MedStars acts as hands-on mentor capitalist to bring to early stage healthcare companies the financial and business expertise needed to get these start-ups off the ground. We work closely with the entrepreneurs to prove technical and market feasibility develop sound business models attract permanent senior management and bring in angel or venture capital financing.
company_description:
HealthTech Capital is a group of private investors dedicated to funding and mentoring new "HealthTech" start ups at the intersection of healthcare and the computer/consumer electronic world. 
Our goal is to invest and mentor capital efficient companies that use new scalable business model to improve the efficiency of healthcare delivery from the hospital to the home.


HealthTech is now an exploding healthcare market that leverages information technologies to decrease healthcare cost. Historically venture funding has focused on "Efficacy" (therapeutics drugs and medical devices) and paid little attention to improving "Efficiencies" of healthcare delivery. Today new paradigm shifting companies like Google Microsoft Intel Apple Amazon and others are turning their attention to HealthTech.

HealthTech supports patient-centric healthcare and is distinct from Healthcare IT enterprise backbone legacy information systems that are well served by existing major players. Our goal is to invest in capital efficient companies that use new business models to improve the efficiency of healthcare delivery.
headline: HealthTech Pioneer as Investor and Innovator Founder and Managing Director @ HealthTech Capital Mentor Capitalist Healthcar

about: Paul Bresge has been a leader in the cell and gene therapy biotech space since 2010. He is co-founder and CEO at Ray Therapeutics a clinical-stage optogenetics gene therapy company for blinding diseases. Prior to co-founding Ray Therapeutics Paul served as CEO at jCyte a late-stage clinical cell therapy company developing a treatment for retinitis pigmentosa. He was involved with jCyte prior to its inception and concluded a $252M licensing deal with Santen Pharmaceutical in 2020. In addition to his current role as CEO Paul is on the board of directors of Healios an iPSC company with programs in ophthalmology and oncology and is a mentor on the Winning Pitch Challenge. Paul is passionate about supporting companies with emerging technologies that have great potential to address unmet needs for patients with blinding and other diseases.
current_position_2: Member of the Board of Directors of Healios KK
company_description:
Founded in 2021 Ray Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing best-in-class vision restoration therapies for people with degenerative retinal diseases independent of genetic mutation. We use the innovative technology of optogenetics to reprogram surviving neurons in the retina of the eye to sense light and send visual signals to the brain—restoring vision.

We are advancing our pipeline of optogenetic medicines to address a range of degenerative retinal diseases with programs in both preclinical and clinical stages of development. Ray Therapeutics' lead candidate RTX-015 is bioengineered to repurpose retinal ganglion cells to restore vision and is being evaluated in a Phase 1 trial in Retinitis Pigmentosa and Choroideremia.

about:
SCIENTIFIC
My expertise includes structure-function analysis of a broad range of proteins and drug targets (kinases proteases antibody-antigen complexes nuclear receptors ubiquitin ligases methylases polymerases microbiome enzymes membrane proteins transcription factors).

TECHNICAL
My core strengths are acquisition processing analysis of big sets of X-ray diffraction/structural genomics data implementation & integration of of high-throughput automation and methods leading multi-site collaborations of discovery projects and fragment- and structure-based drug discovery. Prior to my current role in Accelero Biostructures as a Structural Biologist/Protein crystallographer in the HT Structural Biology/Structural Genomics Gene to Structure pipeline at JCSG that resulted in > 1400 new structures I worked on ~150 new structures and analysis and QC on another ~150 structures. My experience includes protein structure solution by X-ray crystallography protein expression purification protein engineering crystallization synchrotron data collection structural biochemical bioinformatics computational biology biophysical & biomolecular interaction analysis/assays and molecular/ligand screening/modeling for drug discovery. My training also includes chemistry computational chemistry and computer programming in Python R Perl C Pascal.

MANAGEMENT BUSINESS & IP:
Prior to my current role at Accelero Biostructures on drug discovery with many pharma and biotech companies at JCSG I initiated & managed > 10 key partnerships with significant publications. I took Business & Professional Development courses in Stanford Continuing Studies Program. (Startups: From Idea to IPO Marketing for the Non-marketing Professional Strategic Marketing of High Tech Products and Innovations Project Management Creating Executable Business Strategies Creating Effective Customer Acquisition Strategies Business Metrics and Models Thinking Like a Lawyer Patent Paradox).

company_description:
Accelero Biostructures accelerates structure-based drug discovery and protein engineering using its Synchrotron-integrated platform for High-Throughput Protein X-ray Crystallography. With a 15-year track record we are geared toward structure-based drug discovery for pharmaceutical/biotechnology structure-based protein engineering for industrial enzymes and academic drug discovery and structural biology projects.

We capitalize on 15 years of HT protein x-ray crystal structure determination at the Joint Center for Structural Genomics (JCSG) headed by Prof. Ian A. about:
Imagine a world where disease could be predicted before it happens instead of diagnosed and cured by drugs or surgery. Where doctors could use an array of personalized diagnostic tools enabled by predictive analytics data science and AI called precision medicine or digital health. Where doctors can change a patient’s life style diet and wellness regimen and prevent the disease.

The future of healthcare is predictive medicine and the engine that is driving this is a new generation of point-of-care medical devices called Prognostic Devices devices that predict disease rather than just diagnose them. This is the mission of mFluiDx and my other startups. We are developing a highly sensitive ultra-low cost portable and cloud-based diagnostics device for rapid DNA/RNA testing of infectious diseases.

With superior compute performance and network design a comprehensive choice of AI services for developing and orchestrating agentic AI workflows at scale and unrivaled data control security privacy and governance OCI is designed for AI workloads. It also gives customers the flexibility to run their workloads wherever they need by supporting public cloud multicloud hybrid cloud sovereign and on-premises deployments.

The Oracle AI Database is the only database with AI natively built in everywhere. By bringing AI to where data lives the Oracle AI Database delivers enterprise-grade AI securely cost-effectively and at scale. In-database machine learning and vector search enable AI models to run where data resides. Support for multi-modal data (structured unstructured graph vector) enables richer AI use cases. 

The Oracle AI Data Platform enables organizations to build deploy and govern AI agents and applications on top of private secure and distributed enterprise data. This enables organizations to operationalize AI without compromising data security or control. 

Oracle Applications embed AI directly into business workflows and industry processes to ensure AI is delivered in context where work happens. They include the only applications suite that brings AI to every aspect of a business with AI-powered ERP HCM and CX applications and the Oracle Health Suite which provides applications for managing the entire healthcare ecosystem. I help founders who don’t struggle to get the first investor meeting. They struggle to get the second.

Most founders talk to 200+ investors and spend 6+ months to close a round. The difference between those who close fast and those who get stuck? Clarity and credibility.

I’ve spent 15 years helping startups and funds raise capital by shaping their stories through the eyes of investors. My focus is on one critical turning point: when meetings start but don’t convert.

Here’s how I fix that:

• Clarity: I help you build a case for your expertise that investors want to back—fast.
• Credibility: I align your problem solution and business model with what investors see as fundable today.

Fundraising isn’t about more calls or bigger decks. It’s about making investors see you as the right founder for the right idea at the right time.

DM me about your challenge in fundraising.

company_description:
BioCrowd Introduction Video
https://youtu.be/-OiK9MOVxLs

What we do:
We provide capital and resources that accelerate intellectual property (IP) innovation in medical research as an investment vehicle. Help to bridge the gap from grants to institutional funding known as the “Valley of Death”.

BioCrowd provides a collaborative forum and community to help democratize how clinical research is funded how knowledge is shared and the capital and resources that accelerate and monetize intellectual property (IP) innovation and an easier distribution of life changing ownership/royalties for biotech and healthcare innovations along the life of the IP.

Through our biotech accelerator platform scientists and researchers can find funding faster connect directly with patients and other experts and ultimately accelerate the discovery of potential lifesaving new drugs. And this is done with a more iterative economic alignment. 

We provide a marketplace for investing in IP rights and for future cash flows as an issuer and an investment vehicle using SEC-compliant private securities. 

Our startup team at BioCrowd is developing a decentralized open-science (DeSci) marketplace accelerator that is innovating with an equity or royalty tokenized blockchain platform connecting leading early-stage companies and medical researchers with investors community and patients.

Companies and projects have an opportunity to raise capital reward early collaborators or patient groups with valuable tokens by equity or revenue share royalties and/or obtain non-dilutive funding.

We are looking for projects to apply for funding now and also apply as an expert or as a contributor.
headline: Medical knowledge doubles every 73 days. But the infrastructure and capital for funding that progress still moves like it’s 1995

He currently serves as Director of the Blackstone Launchpad powered by Techstars as well as QB3 Entrepreneur in Residence and Rachel Carson Lecturer at the University of California Santa Cruz where he teaches Hacking for Oceans. He has been named a World Economic Forum Global Shaper member of the Fast Company Impact Council MIT Technology Review Innovators Under 35 Ambassador and Judge member of the Review Committee for the America's Seed Fund of the National Science Foundation member of the Partnership on AI Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts Manufactures and Commerce and Delegate to the UN Science Technology & Innovation Forum for the Sustainable Development Goals. 

Previously he served as Founding Emergence Fellow Design Thinking Fellow and Resident Entrepreneur at Stanford and founded the sustainable food biotech startup Vita the sharing economy startup ArtsUp and the sustainability innovation studio GYE. He also conducted research and lectured about technology innovation human-centered design and entrepreneurship for sustainability and societal health at Stanford the University of Milan Bicocca CERN the European Commission Bocconi and more. Andrea chaired the Impact Entrepreneurship Initiative at President Obama's Global Entrepreneurship Summit and advocated for the creation of the Sustainable Development Goals at the UN. He was an EU Marie Curie Fellow in emerging technology for sustainability and health and helped shape Future Earth a global sustainability research and innovation initiative announced at the UN Rio+20 Summit. He also co-created an EU Knowledge Alliance aimed at spurring innovation and entrepreneurship in European universities and inspired CERN to launch CollSpotting a data analytics platform helping science labs and companies to increase their technology transfer to society. He has been a speaker at institutions such as the World Economic Forum European Commission Expo World Ocean Summit and UN and his work and ideas have been featured by publications such as Reuters Fortune Huffington Post and Fast Company.

company_description:
We are a late-stage clinical biotechnology company with a mission to make degenerative brain disorders history. Our robust portfolio of therapies is focused on counteracting the devastating progression of neurodegenerative diseases particularly in areas of high unmet need where therapeutic options are limited. We are at the forefront of a scientific and clinical revolution committed to understanding the complex mechanisms that drive neurodegenerative diseases including the roles of toxic misfolded proteins deficient proteins and lysosomal immune system and neuronal dysfunction. 

We aim to develop product candidates that remove toxic proteins replace critical deficient proteins and restore immune and nerve cells to normal function. To pursue this aim we are advancing a portfolio of programs that address genetically validated targets. These programs leverage our deep understanding of the genetic underpinnings of these diseases combined with our expertise in drug development proprietary protein engineering antibody discovery and our innovative Alector Brain Carrier (ABC) technology for blood-brain barrier (BBB) transport. We are advancing ABC our proprietary versatile BBB technology platform and selectively applying it within our portfolio. 

Alector is based in South San Francisco CA. We were founded in 2013 by thought leaders in biotechnology neuroscience and antibody drug discovery.
